# Grafloria

> The framework-agnostic diagram engine. Flow charts, dashboards, UML, ERD, and real-time
> collaboration — one headless TypeScript core with native bindings for Angular, React, Vue,
> and a plain web component. MIT licensed, every feature free; there is no commercial tier.

Grafloria ships what other diagram libraries sell or leave to DIY: obstacle-avoiding
orthogonal edge routing (never crosses its own nodes), built-in auto-layout (ELK layered,
tree, force — off-thread in a Worker), command-based undo/redo, minimap/snap/marquee editor
chrome, per-property CRDT collaboration with live cursors and anchored comments, keyboard
accessibility with live-region narration, server-side SVG/PNG/PDF export, a Mermaid-compatible
text format that round-trips through the live canvas, a drag-and-pack dashboard kit, and
interactive UML/ER kits. Every capability is demonstrated by one of 100+ live demos, each
executed in CI with real pointer events.

## Packages (npm, all MIT)

- @grafloria/engine — headless diagram model: nodes, edges, ports, groups, layout, CRDT collab. Runs in Node, workers, browsers; no DOM required.
- @grafloria/renderer — SVG renderer over the engine: interaction, routing, editor chrome, export, comments UI.
- @grafloria/angular — Angular components: <grafloria-diagram-canvas> with [(nodes)]/[(edges)] two-way bindings, ng-template custom nodes, [layout], [plugins], [collab], [comments].
- @grafloria/react — React components & hooks: <GrafloriaFlow>, nodeTypes, useGrafloria/useSelection/useViewport, <GrafloriaCommentPanel>.
- @grafloria/vue — Vue 3 components & composables: <GrafloriaFlow> with v-model:nodes, #node-<type> slots, provider composables.
- @grafloria/element — dependency-free custom element <grafloria-flow>; also renders kit specs (erDiagram, umlDiagram, dashboard) and Mermaid-style text.

## Quick start (plain web)

```html
<script type="module">
  import '@grafloria/element';
</script>
<grafloria-flow id="flow"></grafloria-flow>
<script>
  document.getElementById('flow').render({
    nodes: [
      { id: 'a', label: 'Start', x: 40,  y: 60 },
      { id: 'b', label: 'Ship',  x: 260, y: 60 },
    ],
    edges: [{ id: 'e1', source: 'a', target: 'b' }],
  });
</script>
```

## Quick start (React)

```tsx
import { GrafloriaFlow } from '@grafloria/react';

<GrafloriaFlow
  nodes={nodes}
  edges={edges}
  onNodesChange={setNodes}
  layout="elk"
  plugins
/>
```

## Docs

- [Live demo gallery](https://grafloria.com/demos/): 100+ runnable pages — view-source is the tutorial; every demo is also a CI test.
- [How Grafloria compares](https://grafloria.com/compare/): factual matrix vs React Flow, JointJS+, GoJS, ngx-vflow, Mermaid.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/grafloria/grafloria): source, READMEs per package, issues.
- [npm organization](https://www.npmjs.com/org/grafloria): published packages.

## Notes for code generation

- Ports are model anatomy (spec.ports on a node), not separate components — identical across all frameworks.
- The same JSON spec drives every surface; there is no framework-specific model.
- Collab: pass collab={{ transport, actor, presence: true }} — the CRDT lives in the engine; BroadcastChannel works serverless for same-browser tabs.
- Text format: instance.exportText() / instance.loadText() round-trip Mermaid-style text with a lossless position sidecar.
- Dashboards / UML / ER: pass a kit spec ({ dashboard: ... }, { umlDiagram: ... }, { erDiagram: ... }) to the same render/spec entry points.
